name: Momentum/breakout filtered by MLOFI
hypothesis: Breakouts confirmed by persistent five-level OFI should continue better than price-only breakouts.
required_scenarios: gradual trend; strong rally; sell-off; false breakout; sideways; shock continuation; shock reversal
primary_metrics: false_breakout_reduction; net_expectancy; mae_mfe; cost_sensitivity; latency_tolerance; regime_stability
caveat: Current proxy uses 5-minute synthetic features, not true 1/5/15/30 second MLOFI lookbacks.

name: Pure multi-level OFI directional model
hypothesis: Five-level OFI should predict future mid-price movement beyond static imbalance.
caveat: Current proxy can compare static imbalance and mlofi_qty at 5-minute event spacing only.

name: Liquidity-vacuum breakout
hypothesis: One-sided depth withdrawal plus aggressive flow should precede a short-horizon move.
required_scenarios: genuine continuation; fake withdrawal; spread blowout; market shock; isolated vacuum; rapid recovery; multi-level sweep
caveat: Only a weak proxy exists because Phase 9 Tier C does not yet carry explicit withdrawal/replenishment rates.

name: Trade-flow plus depth confirmation
hypothesis: Trade imbalance and depth flow jointly classify continuation, absorption, exhaustion and false breakout.
caveat: Event intensity is not a substitute for signed aggressive trade imbalance.

name: Microprice entry/exit filter
hypothesis: Microprice should improve next-move estimates and entry/exit timing.
caveat: Standalone microprice results are diagnostic only; plan says not to promote it as a standalone annual-return strategy.

name: Absorption and exhaustion reversal
hypothesis: Large aggressive flow with limited price progress followed by reversal should predict short-term reversal.
required_scenarios: true absorption; pause before continuation; hidden-liquidity-like replenishment; post-shock stabilization; deceptive replenishment
caveat: Can only label absorption-like proxies; top-five market-by-price cannot prove iceberg/participant identity.

name: Mean reversion after imbalance
hypothesis: Extreme imbalance followed by replenishment and lower intensity should predict reversion in non-trend regimes.
required_scenarios: sideways; high-volatility sideways; post-shock recovery; sustained trend; panic; rally
caveat: Proxy uses imbalance/intensity and regime_code; explicit replenishment is still missing.

name: Cross-ticker/index lead-lag OFI
hypothesis: Market, sector or leader OFI should improve follower prediction while avoiding simultaneous-shock false positives.
required_scenarios: true lead-lag; simultaneous shock; timestamp skew; spurious correlation; changing leader; no lead-lag
caveat: Can compute market-level proxy factors, but causal lead-lag validation requires dedicated synthetic controls.

name: Pure queue-imbalance scalping
hypothesis: Queue imbalance may predict next quote direction but must survive latency and costs.
caveat: Benchmark only. Accuracy above 50% is not sufficient without spread/cost survival.

name: Passive market making
hypothesis: Synthetic testing can develop inventory/risk logic but cannot validate real queue fills from shallow MBP data.
required_features: queue_position; partial_fills; cancellation_latency; inventory; adverse_selection; liquidation_cost
required_scenarios: optimistic fills; neutral fills; pessimistic fills
caveat: Requires Phase 12 execution simulator and pessimistic queue-fill assumptions.

name: Spoof-like wall filter
hypothesis: Wall appearance/cancellation patterns can be used only as a risk feature, not participant classification.
required_scenarios: temporary wall; genuine liquidity; shock liquidity; no wall
caveat: Current top-five snapshots do not support spoof/manipulation assertions.
